explicit_rule_overrides.reason: Internal file and function comment requirements override the external minimalist comment guidance.
Internal comment requirements define when comments are mandatory.
Internal indentation, brace, and spacing rules are authoritative.
Altibase naming conventions intentionally encode module and type prefixes.
Internal naming style is authoritative.
Altibase forbids underscores in variable and user-defined type names, which conflicts with underscore_style guidance.
Internal spacing rules are more specific and authoritative.
Internal include and header wrapper rules override external source-file guidance.
Internal wrapper-based include policy overrides direct standard header guidance.
Internal include syntax and wrapper-header rules override quoted-vs-angle include guidance.
Internal brace and formatting rules take precedence.
Internal spacing policy takes precedence.
Internal statement-per-line policy takes precedence.
Internal convention requires explicit void for functions that take no arguments.
Altibase forbids declaring variables in the for-statement initializer.
Internal enum and macro naming rules authorize uppercase identifiers in specific cases.
Altibase relies on approved macro systems and treats their usage as part of the internal convention.
Internal macro-based infrastructure overrides the external no-macro recommendation.
Internal convention explicitly requires NULL after free().
Altibase for-loop restrictions override the preference for range-for when it would violate the local convention.
Altibase forbids declarations in the for initializer.
Altibase prefers a single exit point near the end of a function.

keyword_overrides.reason: Internal naming, comment, and include rules are authoritative.
Internal portability and type-system rules are authoritative.
Internal identifier conventions override general naming-style guidance.
Internal macro conventions override external anti-macro guidance.
Internal loop-variable placement rules are authoritative.
